data_as_of: 2026-07-21 session close (live TradingView feed, Cboe One, bar ts 1784640600)
market_lines: Three-session losing streak snapped. Read the shape before the headline: this was a gap-up that barely extended.

Open 746.29 / high 749.04 / low 744.18. Open-to-close only +0.27% — the overnight gap did the work, the session did almost nothing. Weak-quality strength; argues against calling this a confirmed reversal.
Open 706.57 / low 702.80. Same shape as SPY, +0.34% open-to-close.
Open 293.43. +1.06% open-to-close — roughly 4x SPY's. Small caps were the real intraday leader on a day the headline was megacap chips. A genuine broadening would show up here first.
Traded down to 572.05 and closed 584.08 = +2.10% off the low. After weeks of bear-market damage, the first real demand response. Best single tell in today's tape.
Directionally consistent with the ETFs above.
SPY above its 50- and 200-day with the 200 rising (scanner, on 7/20 data).
Middling, not thrust-like. 49% of 1,167 names in Stage 2.
MU ~+12%, SNDK ~+14%, INTC and AMD also double-digit. This is a memory-cycle trade, NOT a re-acceleration of the AI-capex complex. The distinction matters for the ignition work.

DHR -11% (beat, weak bioprocessing), MSCI -10% (missed elevated expectations), EFX -3.9% (beat, cut guidance), WDAY -4.1% (downgrade). GM beat EPS AND raised full-year guidance and still fell 1.3%. Beats are not being paid for — only guidance is.
Adj EPS $2.40 on $6.5B, organic +5.4%, ~25% operating margin, raised 2026 guidance. The one place a beat got paid.

ITA touched 232.08 and closed 229.74, giving back ~1% off the high — consistent with the March-playbook defense-fade flag. The day's defense strength was UK, not US (Babcock +7.3%, BAE +3.3%) on the new UK cabinet.
Settled LOWER after Qatar and Pakistan floated a 10-day ceasefire proposal, despite having been above $90 intraday. WTI 85.10 overnight.

THE KEY ASYMMETRY: tankers rallied on a day crude FELL. That is the thesis decoupling from crude beta and trading on rates and disruption — the more durable driver. VLCC day rates ~$170k/day, roughly tripled since January.
Iran struck a tanker in Hormuz this morning, crew abandoned ship. Two Saudi crude cargoes bound for Asia reversed course in the Red Sea. Disruption is physical, not rhetorical.

FOMC July 28-29. Hold fell 7.05 pts on the week; the +25bp hike rose 7.40 pts to 14.45%. Cut tail is dead at 0.60%. Kevin Warsh now chairs and has called inflation 'too high'. Oil is the transmission channel.
One cut fell to 10.5%. Easing keeps getting pushed further out.
The market reads the oil shock as supply/inflation, NOT demand destruction. That argues for being long the shock (tankers, energy) rather than short the cycle.
With WTI at 85.10 and ten days left, a coin flip on $90 and only ~13% to $100 — materially less bullish than Goldman's $120-if-Hormuz-persists tail.

MOST ACTIONABLE READ FOR THE TANKER BOOK: near-term de-escalation is heavily discounted, so the disruption premium holds through July — then the odds flip against it. The tanker trade has a ~3-6 week window. Time-box it; this is the argument for the ceasefire kill-switch.

Chubb: core operating $7.26/sh vs Street $6.60-6.77, +18.2% y/y, combined ratio 83.8%, BVPS +12.3% — but net premiums written only +3.6%, so growth is the soft spot. Triumph Financial reported but actuals are not yet retrievable; Street was $0.44 on $114.7M.
CB and TFIN press releases are titled July 22 because that is the CALL date — the releases dropped tonight, 7/21. Cost nothing today only because both were already blocked.
Today's origination scan is one session stale — verified across all 29 names. Seven names it flagged as in-zone had already run out of them. Anything ignited TODAY was never screened.
rotation_commentary: ROTATION RADAR DID NOT RUN TONIGHT — python execution was blocked this session, so scripts/rotation_radar.py could not execute. Any radar table above is from a PREVIOUS run and may be stale. Owed tomorrow.
Manual rotation read from the feed and the tape:

· INTO: memory semiconductors (MU, SNDK, INTC, AMD) — but read this as a memory-cycle move, not a broad AI-semis re-acceleration. SMH's +2.10% recovery off its low is the first real demand response after weeks of distribution.
· INTO: small caps. IWM led intraday at +1.06% open-to-close vs SPY's +0.27%. If the broadening is real it shows here first — worth a specific check tomorrow.
· INTO: energy/tankers, on disruption rather than crude. NAT +5.03% and STNG firm on a day Brent fell 1.4%. The decoupling is the signal.
· OUT OF: anything that reported without raising. DHR -11%, MSCI -10%, EFX -3.9%, WDAY -4.1%, and GM -1.3% despite beating AND raising. The market is paying for guidance only.
· OUT OF / FADING: US defense. ITA faded ~1% off its intraday high, consistent with the March-playbook defense-fade call. UK defense was the day's strength instead.

· UNRESOLVED: healthcare. Feed shows XLV opened 157.53 and closed 160.25 — up on the day — which directly contradicts the -1.2% figure circulating in today's press recaps (those describe the JULY 20 session). Do not reuse those sector percentages.

NOT VERIFIED: full 11-sector daily percentages. The feed gives closes and intraday direction, not change-vs-prior-close for every SPDR, and no reputable same-day source was reachable. Treat precise sector percentages as unconfirmed.
IGNITION SWEEP DID NOT RUN — scripts/ignition_sweep.py also blocked. No market-wide ignition pass tonight; the before-photo detector and convergence rule were not applied.
plans: THE ONLY SURVIVOR of the O.G Chandelier gate, and it survives with a conflict. CE is in buy mode and the 6.55 buy-stop triggers ABOVE ZLSMA 6.54, so the structure is sound — but Magical reads 125.0 (>+100 = too hot, pullback-only) and the CE buy label at 5.32 is ~22% back, so the flip is old. Momentum rule says wait; structure rule says the breakout is valid. Half size is the compromise. CEASEFIRE KILL-SWITCH APPLIES: ceasefire 18.5% by 7/31 but 55% by 8/31, so this is a 3-6 week time-boxed trade, not a hold.
SELL MODE (sell label 57.03), price 53.95 below ZLSMA 59.65. Rule 10 veto. This was the #1 plan before the indicator gate ran.
SELL MODE (sell label 35.09), price below ZLSMA 30.75. Rule 10 veto.
SELL MODE (sell label 5.61). Rule 10 veto — and it had already been flagged as falling INTO its zone rather than bouncing.
Magical 139.5 = too hot, and price 47.21 sits below ZLSMA 47.71. CE buy label 33.45 is ~41% back — an old flip, not a fresh ignition.
Magical 176.1 — the hottest reading in the candidate set. Trend structure is fine (above ZLSMA 41.32) but buying here is chasing. Re-qualifies on a pullback toward 41.30.

Best structural read of the 76. CE buy, price 970.82 well above ZLSMA 838.04, and Magical only -22.8 — it ran +12.17% today WITHOUT going overbought on this oscillator. Flip is old (label 761.36, ~27% back). Memory-semis leadership.
Freshest signal in the entire scan by the distance proxy: CE buy label 217.81 is only ~5.5% below the 229.86 close, the tightest gap of all 76. Above ZLSMA 220.26, Magical a cool 22.7.
CE buy, above ZLSMA 70.63, Magical -30.4 leaves room. Closed +4.88% on the gold move. Cleanest of the ETF passers.
CE buy, above ZLSMA 131.96, Magical 9.0 = cold. Caveat: only 215K volume.
CE buy, price 127.39 far above ZLSMA 106.37, Magical -68.1 despite closing +6.24%.
CE buy, just above ZLSMA 42.17, Magical 11.4 = cold. Marginal on the ZLSMA gap.
CE buy, above ZLSMA 176.59, Magical 79.1 approaching hot. Closed +2.34%.
CE buy, above ZLSMA 26.26, Magical 84.2 warm but under the line. Prior work flagged the KWEB engine as real but watch-only.
CE buy, above ZLSMA 377.30, but Magical 96.1 sits right under the +100 too-hot threshold.
CE buy and just above ZLSMA 266.42, but the buy label at 133.64 is ~103% below current price — the oldest live signal in the scan.
The one origination name that passes the stack. But it closed +3.37% ABOVE its buy-zone top of 15.73, so entry now is a chase. Watch 15.12-15.73.

The indicator gate cut committed risk from $25,829 to $2,500. Five of six prior plans were vetoed — three of them outright SELL-MODE vetoes. No candidate below has been earnings-verified or sized yet; none should be armed until it is.
